seoTitle: Embed YouTube, Instagram, Facebook & X in GoHighLevel
metaDescription: Learn how to embed YouTube videos, Instagram posts, Facebook content, and X posts directly into your GoHighLevel website.
intro: Embedding social media content directly into your GoHighLevel website helps showcase your videos, posts, and social proof without sending visitors away from your site. This overview covers the general approach to embedding YouTube, Instagram, Facebook, and X content into your pages.

steps.heading: Choose the content you want to embed
steps.body: Decide which YouTube video, Instagram post, Facebook content, or X post you want to feature on your website.

steps.heading: Get the embed code or link
steps.body: Retrieve the embed code or shareable link from the source platform for the content you want to display.

steps.heading: Add an embed element to your page
steps.body: Use GoHighLevel's page builder to insert an embed or custom code block where the content should appear.

steps.heading: Paste and configure the embed
steps.body: Add the embed code or link into the block and adjust sizing or styling so it fits your page layout.

steps.heading: Preview and publish
steps.body: Check how the embedded content displays before publishing the page to make sure it renders correctly.

faq.question: Can I embed content from multiple platforms on the same page?
faq.answer: Yes, you can add separate embed blocks for YouTube, Instagram, Facebook, and X content on the same page.

faq.question: Do I need coding knowledge to embed social content?
faq.answer: Basic embedding is typically done by pasting a code or link into a builder block, which doesn't require deep coding knowledge.

faq.question: Will embedded content slow down my page?
faq.answer: Embeds can add some load time depending on the platform, so it's worth testing your page's performance after adding them.

faq.question: Can I embed a live social feed instead of a single post?
faq.answer: This depends on the specific embed option used; some setups support live feeds while others display a single piece of content.

faq.question: Why would I embed social content instead of just linking to it?
faq.answer: Embedding keeps visitors on your site while still showcasing your social proof and content, rather than sending them away to another platform.
